name: theHarvester
purpose: OSINT aggregator for emails, subdomains, and hostnames from public sources
author: Christian Martorella (laramies)
why_notable: Written by Spanish researcher Christian Martorella around 2008 to automate the tedium of manually Googling a target's email addresses across multiple search engines. It became the canonical OSINT aggregator after being bundled into BackTrack Linux around 2010 — once in BackTrack, every pentest course and certification (OSCP, CEH, SANS) referenced it, making it the industry default. The v4 rewrite (2019) introduced async HTTP via aiohttp and 40+ source modules, each as a separate Python class. It's most useful with API keys configured in api-keys.yaml; without them, Google blocks scraping and many sources silently fail.
